segments.text: Here's something that I've never shared publicly\h before. I make between $10,000 and $35,000 a month\h\h
of profit on Facebook sharing content that is\h mostly not my own. I do this legally, ethically,\h\h
and it only takes me about 3 hours per week.\h Why have I never talked about this? Honestly,\h\h
because I haven't wanted anyone to steal this\h from me. But then I thought, what the heck,\h\h
Chris? That's not your brand. Law of abundance,\h right? Let's show people exactly how they can do\h\h
this for themselves. and surely the universe\h will send me some good stuff in return. So,\h\h
this is going to be very tactical. I'm going\h to share my screen, show you exactly what I do,\h\h
show you exactly how you can do it, and I'm going\h to show you why this is even an opportunity in the\h\h
first place. You don't need to be an influencer.\h You don't need to come up with original ideas.\h\h
You just need to have a brain and to use it. The\h reason this is an opportunity is because Facebook\h\h
has 2.1 billion daily active users, but they don't\h have enough creators publishing good content that\h\h
these two billion people can actually consume\h on a daily basis. So, the supply and demand is\h\h
imbalanced. Therefore, they need to pay creators\h to make good content. Whereas on Instagram,\h\h
there's plenty of creators and plenty of daily\h active users. So, they don't need to pay creators.\h\h
So, we're not talking about Instagram today. We're\h talking about Facebook. Whether you're 5 years old\h\h
or 500 years old, you can make money doing this.\h I promise you. And if you stick around to the end,\h\h
I will show you how you can make way, way, way\h more money by driving that Facebook traffic off\h\h
of Facebook. Because the real money in content\h creation is not in relying on other platforms\h\h

here. So, I'm going to show you exactly\h how all that works at the end as well.

daily updates on Facebook with our friends. It's\h all algorithmically driven. Most of those updates\h\h
happen on Instagram now. But old habits die hard.\h That's why over a quarter of the planet is using\h\h
Facebook every single day. But the people that\h used to post on Facebook are no longer posting.\h\h
